rulings.Case Title: KEVIN CALLEN II VS. HENSEL PHELPS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Admissions, Interrogatories, And Requests For Production
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, February 19, 2019, Line 1, PLAINTIFF KEVIN CALLEN's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Admissions, Interrogatories, And Requests For Production Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. The parties shall address the adequacy and completeness of the Supplemental Responses to the subject discovery served after the filing of the within motion. The Court will rule on remaining objections, if necessary. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@sbslawsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: LINDA VARGAS VS. DIGNITY HEALTH ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT ;Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, February 19, 2019, Line 2, DEFENDANT ARAMARK HEALTHCARE SUPPORT SERVICES, LLC MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT ;Memorandum Of Points And Authorities Defendant Aramark's motion for summary judgment is denied. Even if Aramark was not assigned to clean the bathrooms while AMB's construction work was ongoing, it still assumed the duty to do so. (Mony Depo., 76:8-13; Gregg Depo., 60:3-15.) When plaintiff entered the bathroom, she could tell that construction was still ongoing. (Vargas Depo., 29:3-6.) AMB's work included dusty drywall and tile work. (Mony Depo., 153:10-156:13.) In addition, immediately prior to the accident, drilling occurred in the ceiling of the women's bathroom to install anchors and the drilling would create dust. (Exs. 21 and 22; Perez Dec.) A reasonable trier of fact could infer that Aramark assumed the duty to clean the bathroom and there is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether Ms. Gregg breached that duty. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/rbu

rulings.Case Title: LINDA VARGAS VS. DIGNITY HEALTH ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication On Behalf Of Dignity Health
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, February 19, 2019, Line 2, DEFENDANT DIGNITY HEALTH's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication On Behalf Of Dignity Health Defendant Dignity Health's motion for summary judgment or alternatively summary adjudication is denied. Defendant does not maintain its initial burden of production. Defendant fails to show that the fine dust on the light colored floor was a trivial defect and therefore there is a triable issue of material fact regarding the existence of a dangerous condition of property. (Vargas Special Rog. Response, #1.) Privette v. Sup. Crt. (1993) 5 Cal.4th 689 and its progeny are inapposite. The instant action does not involve the injury of an employee of a contractor. This case does not implicate the fairness/worker's compensation issues of Privette. Dignity fails to show that it did not create the dangerous condition. (See Getchell v. Rogers Jewelry (2012) 203 Cal.App.4th 381, 385.) In fact, Dignity performed HVAC/plumbing work in the bathroom shortly before the accident. (Traum Dec., Ex. 3., Mony Depo., 107:3-109:20.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/rbu

rulings.Case Title: VATCHE CABAYAN M.D ET AL VS. STATE COMPENSATION INSURANCE FUND ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Proceeding And Petition For Relief Or, In The Alternative Transfer, For Lack Of Subject Matter Jurisdiction And Supporting Memorandum
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, February 19, 2019, Line 8, DEFENDANT STATE COMPENSANTION INSURANCE FUND, A PUBLIC's Motion To Dismiss Proceeding And Petition For Relief Or, In The Alternative Transfer, For Lack Of Subject Matter Jurisdiction And Supporting Memorandum Defendant State Compensation Insurance Fund's motion to dismiss proceeding and its petition for relief or, in the alternative, transfer for lack of subject matter jurisdiction are DENIED without prejudice to renewal after the second amended complaint is filed. Absent an operative complaint, I would largely be ruling in a vacuum. Though State Fund did not file a motion for protective order within 30 days of the court's November 6, 2018 order, it did file one on February 4, 2019. The court's November order gave plaintiffs discretion to await a ruling on the proposed protective order-a March 29, 2019 hearing is set-before filing their second amended complaint. That accomplished, the court presumably will have the information needed to consider State Fund's motion and petition. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: RICHARD SPENCER VS. GENERAL MOTORS LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, February 19, 2019, Line 9, DEFENDANT GENERAL MOTORS LLC's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT General Motors, LLC's demurrer to the fraud by omission cause of action is OVERRULED. The amended complaint alleges sufficient facts to invoke the discovery rule and therefore the claim is not facially time-barred. (Fox v. Ethicon Endo-Surgery, Inc. (2005) 35 Cal.4th 797, 808; Amended Complaint, 42.) Plaintiff's allegations of exclusive knowledge and active concealment support the claim in the absence of a fiduciary relationship. (See Heliotis v. Schuman (1986) 181 Cal.App.3d 646, 651; Amended Complaint, 12-15 and 102.) The economic loss rule does not bar the claim because plaintiff pleads tortious conduct independent of the alleged breach of contract. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: ARTERRA MISSION BAY, LLC VS. BOVIS LEND LEASE, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion In Support Of Arterra Mission Bay, Llcs Motion For Preliminary Injunction, Or, In The Alternative, To Stay Arbitration And For Consolidation
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, February 19, 2019, Line 11, PLAINTIFF ARTERRA MISSION BAY, LLC 's Motion In Support Of Arterra Mission Bay, Llcs Motion For Preliminary Injunction, Or, In The Alternative, To Stay Arbitration And For Consolidation Arterra's motion for preliminary injunction is DENIED. The Association and JAMS are not parties to this action. In fact, Arterra seeks injunctive relief against non-party JAMS and there is no showing that JAMS even has notice of this motion. While in some circumstances an injunction can run against a non-party agent, the court believes that the better practice would be to name JAMS or at the very least give it notice of this motion. (See Hassell v. Bird (2018) 5 Cal.5th 522, 549 ("Consistent with this principle, courts have long observed a general rule against entering injunctions against nonparties.").) In addition, Arterra's complaint states no viable claim for injunctive relief. "A preliminary injunction is an interim remedy designed to maintain the status quo pending a decision on the merits. [citation] It is not, in itself, a cause of action. Thus, a cause of action must exist before injunctive relief may be granted. [citation] Accordingly, where the complaint fails to state a cause of action an order granting a preliminary injunction must be reversed. [citation] (MaJor v. Miraverde Homeowners Assn. (1992) 7 Cal.App.4th 618, 623-24.) "A preliminary injunction is warranted only if there is on file a complaint which states a sufficient cause of action for injunctive relief of the character embraced in the preliminary injunction." (Moreno Mutual Irrigation Co. v. Beaumont Irrigation District (1949) 94 Cal.App.2d 766, 778.) In this case, Artrerra is effectively seeking specific performance and/or injunctive relief with respect to Section 5.3.1 of the CC&Rs without pleading such a claim. In Dennis v. Overholtzer (1957) 149 Cal.App.2d 101, the court provides that the trial court can issue an injunction based on the affidavits, but that was in the context of preserving the status quo among parties to the action. Arterra's alternative motion for a stay or consolidation per CCP 1281.2(c) is DENIED. Arterra fails to show that any of the recently named Does will not agree to arbitration and there exists a possibility of conflicting rulings. (See Bos Material Handling, Inc. v. Crown Controls Corp. (1982) 137 Cal.App.3d 99, 112.) LendLease, a non party to the arbitration agreement, agreed to arbitrate. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the Association is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/rbu
